//: Marriage -> family -> birth content arc (docs/design/marriage_and_conception.md §5), Sonnet 5. All three events are scope: avatar -- only the avatar has a tracked bloodline, and conceive_child is loader-rejected on any other scope. Departs from the design doc's own literal example (`target: league`) for met_someone's partner consequence: uses `target: teammate` instead, the same call career_arc_events.json already made for spring_training_romance -- the league pool spans every Players row with no age floor (an heir of any age is a candidate), while teammate/opponent are engine-guaranteed rostered-and-therefore-adult (LeagueGenerator rolls age 21-36; succession backfill windows to [MaturityAge, MandatoryRetirementAge)). It also guarantees the partner carries a real Player_Ratings row, which is strictly better for CareerManager.ConceiveChild's two-parent blend than an arbitrary league pick. This was flagged explicitly as a judgment call for Sonnet to weigh in the engine-plumbing progress entry's known artifacts; resolved here in favor of teammate. Paced by the min_days_since cascade clock exactly like bribe -> compromised_syndicate -> shakedown: met_someone (partner + married) -> starting_a_family at +365 days (expecting) -> child_is_born at +270 days (conceive_child, clears expecting so the arc can repeat for a second child). HS-5 realism pass (Fable 5): marriage is no longer permanent-until-death -- marriage_on_the_rocks (year-plus marriages, ~1-2 fires/season) can set marriage_strained, and divorce_papers 60+ days later ends it via 'end_partnership' (the Partner edge reclassified friend/rival, never deleted -- exes stay graph history), clearing married so met_someone can eventually fire again (remarriage). Reconciliation paths exist at both beats, so a player who fights for the marriage keeps it; the autopilot default divorces strained marriages 3-to-1, landing career-long divorce odds in the real-life ~half-of-marriages band. Cross-file flags: met_someone now also gates flag_inactive hs_dating (an avatar still with their high-school sweetheart doesn't 'meet someone' -- hs_dating_events.json's marry_hs_sweetheart converts that thread into this file's married flag instead, same Partner edge). A divorce while expecting deliberately leaves expecting set: child_is_born then fires with the co-parent resolved live as null -- a single-parent birth, which CareerManager.ConceiveChild supports by design.

events.prompt: Somewhere between the bus rides and the bullpen small talk, one teammate has become the person you look for first. This feels like more than a clubhouse friendship.
The two of you have started talking about what comes next -- a bigger place, a name you both like, a life built around more than road trips. It doesn't feel like just talk anymore.
Nine months of waiting ends in a hospital room in the middle of the night, and suddenly the contract, the stat line, and the grind all feel very far away. A tiny hand wraps around your finger.
The road trips are longer, the conversations are shorter, and lately the house feels quietest when you're both in it. Something has to give.
A manila envelope is waiting on the kitchen table when you get home from the ballpark. You both knew this was coming; seeing it in writing still knocks the wind out of you.

choices.label: Sign and part on decent terms
Tear it up and try again
